author: y4rr
content:
Phase 1C extension complete. Integrated 9 SciPy/torch.signal.windows extras from ferray-window 0.3.1:

- cosine(m): half-cycle cosine ('sine' window)
- exponential(m, center, tau): exponentially-decaying with optional centre
- gaussian(m, std): Gaussian, configurable std
- general_cosine(m, coeffs): generic cosine-sum (recovers Hann via [0.5, 0.5], classical Blackman via [0.42, 0.5, 0.08])
- general_hamming(m, alpha): alpha-parameterised; alpha=0.5 -> Hann, 0.54 -> NumPy Hamming
- nuttall(m): 4-term minimum-derivative Blackman-Harris
- parzen(m): cubic B-spline
- taylor(m, nbar, sll, norm): Carrara & Goodman; defaults nbar=4, sll=30, norm=true
- tukey(m, alpha): cosine-tapered; alpha=0 rectangular, alpha=1 Hann

Bumped workspace dep ferray-window 0.3 -> 0.3.1 (now published on crates.io). Dropped the temporary [patch.crates-io] block.

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline):
- All new windows return Tensor<f64> on Device::Cpu
- output_lives_on_cpu test extended to cover all 15 windows (5 NumPy core + 9 new + 1 explicit kaiser variant), asserting every entry returns CPU
- No GPU dispatch added for the same reason as the original windows: a 1us coefficient generator + cudaMemcpy is strictly slower than CPU compute + later .to(Device::Cuda)
- Forbidden patterns grep clean

Tests: 22 new in signal::windows::tests covering:
- cosine length + symmetry
- exponential default-centre symmetry, rejects nonpositive tau
- gaussian centre = 1 for odd m, known value at z=1 (exp(-0.5)), rejects nonpositive std
- general_cosine matches Hann ([0.5, 0.5]) and Blackman ([0.42, 0.5, 0.08]); rejects empty coeffs
- general_hamming alpha=0.5 matches Hann, alpha=0.54 matches Hamming
- nuttall length + symmetry, low endpoint magnitude
- parzen centre = 1, symmetric
- taylor normalised centre = 1, symmetric, rejects nbar=0 and NaN sll
- tukey alpha=0 rectangular, alpha=1 matches Hann, centre=1 for moderate alpha, rejects out-of-range alpha

Workspace: 4550 passed, 0 failed, 100 ignored (was 4528; +22 new signal tests + ferray transitive bump 0.3.0 -> 0.3.1 on ferray-core/ufunc/macros). Closes #578.

Resolves the 'less-common windows tracked as future work' note from #566's closeout. ferrotorch's torch.signal.windows surface is now COMPLETE — all 14 entries (the 5 NumPy core + 9 SciPy extras) are wrapped.
